prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for benzaldehyde computed at DFT tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, PySCF RKS, exact four-center integrals — density fitting OFF), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. N−1 cation: charge +1, doublet; N+1 anion: charge −1, doublet. Partial charges via Mulliken (PySCF). Geometry taken as-is from user-supplied .xyz (single-point Fukui, no re-optimization at this level). 14 atoms total.

Key results:
- Most electrophilic site: Atom 3 (C, ring), f⁺ = 0.39938967
- Most nucleophilic site: Atom 1 (O, carbonyl oxygen), f⁻ = 0.23839906
- Largest positive dual descriptor: Atom 3 (C), Δf = +0.36263555 → primary electrophilic attack site
- Aldehyde carbon (atom 2) and all H atoms carry negative dual descriptors

Integrity: status=ok, trustworthy=true. Both fukui_charge_conservation checks (sum(f⁺)=1.0000, sum(f⁻)=1.0000) passed. No warnings reported by the engine.

CLI: python /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --stdout json --accept-defaults --out benzaldehyde_fukui_dft_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json

Output: benzaldehyde_fukui_dft_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json; plot: .../agent_call_01_plot.png
